(a) the Abstract of a Decision;


(b) the Statement of Enforceability; and


(c) the Statement of Proper Notice.


### 7.7


In an application under the 2007 Hague Convention, the certificate will additionally state the jurisdictional basis upon which the order was made, with reference to the jurisdictional criteria in Article 20(1) of that Convention to be applied by the State in which recognition and/or enforcement is to be sought.

To the top

## Countries and Territories in which Sums are Payable through Crown Agents for Overseas Governments and Territories (rule 34.23)


### 8.1


Gibraltar, Barbados, Bermuda, Ghana, Kenya, Fiji, Hong Kong, Singapore, Turks and Caicos Islands, United Republic of Tanzania (except Zanzibar), Anguilla, Falkland Islands and Dependencies, St Helena.

To the top

## Part 1 of the 1972 Act – Modified Rules


### 9.1


The annexes to this Practice Direction set out rules 34.14 to 34.25 as they are modified –


(a) in relation to the Republic of Ireland, by rule 34.26 (Annex 1) (but see the note in that Annex regarding the ongoing relevance of those rules following revocation of rule 34.26);


(b) in relation to the Hague Convention Countries, by rule 34.27 (Annex 2); and


(c) in relation to Specified States of the United States of America, by rule 34.28 (Annex3).


### 9.2


The statutory references in the annexes are construed in accordance with rule 34.26(2), 34.27(2) or 34.28(2) as the case may be.

To the top

## Annex 1 Application of Section 1 of Chapter 2 of Part 34 to the Republic of Ireland
